business_hours.holiday_policy: Observe major holidays but flexible for high-priority clients.

office_address: 2750 South Dale Mabry Highway, Suite 310, Tampa, Florida 33629

contact_info.name: Maria
contact_info.context: First point of contact for emergencies, handles calls during the day and billing/paperwork.

contact_info.name: Tony
contact_info.context: Second point of contact for emergencies, currently receives all after-hours calls on his cell phone.

contact_info.name: Pete
contact_info.context: Last resort for critical emergency calls.

contact_info.name: Maria
contact_info.context: Primary contact for all calls during business hours and Tier 1 for emergencies.

contact_info.name: Tony Russo
contact_info.context: Tier 2 for emergencies and direct transfers for existing customers during business hours.

services_supported: Access control systems (key cards, fobs, biometrics)
Commercial security alarm systems - intrusion detection
Video analytics setup - motion detection zones
Video analytics setup - license plate recognition
services_excluded: Electrical work beyond low-voltage (e.g., new panel, outlet installation)
emergency_definition: Fire alarm system malfunctions causing ongoing false alarms
Complete alarm panel failures (fire or security) leaving a building unprotected
Security system breaches at high-risk facilities (banks, data centers, government buildings)
Monitoring company calls about critical system faults (from ADT, Brinks, or any central station)
CCTV system failures at sites with active security concerns
Access control system lockouts (someone can't get into their building)
Any situation where a client explicitly says it's an emergency related to their life safety systems

order: If Maria is unreachable, call Tony (Tier 2) for 30 seconds
If Tony is unreachable, call Pete (Tier 3) for 30 seconds
emergency_routing_rules.fallback_action: Take a detailed message with urgency level and send text alerts to Maria, Tony, and Pete.

non_emergency_routing_rules.action: Clara to take a detailed message.
non_emergency_routing_rules.notes: Most non-emergency calls are requests for quotes on new installations or requests to schedule service visits. These can wait until the next business day.

call_transfer_rules.during_hours_transfer: Maria at 813-555-0145 (primary). Tony at 813-555-0167 (for specific requests or monitoring calls if Maria is unavailable).
call_transfer_rules.transfer_fail_action: Clara to handle the call, collect information, and Maria will call back.
notes: For existing customers asking for Tony specifically, transfer to Tony's cell (813-555-0167).
For monitoring company calls, try transferring to Maria immediately. If she is unavailable, transfer to Tony.

integration_constraints: Uses Connectwise Manage for ticketing and project management.
No API integration with Clara needed for Connectwise Manage.
Clara should never try to create tickets or access Connectwise.
Clara should NEVER ask for or record alarm codes, passwords, or access credentials. If a client has a code-related question, Clara should note it and route to Maria or Tony.

Service area is the Tampa Bay metropolitan area (Tampa, St. Petersburg, Clearwater, Brandon, and surrounding Hillsborough and Pinellas counties). Calls from outside this area (e.g., Miami, Jacksonville) cannot be serviced.
after_hours_flow_summary: During after-hours, if a call is identified as an emergency based on the defined triggers, Clara will initiate a three-tier escalation process: first calling Maria (813-555-0145) for 30 seconds, then Tony (813-555-0167) for 30 seconds, and finally Pete (813-555-0189) for 30 seconds. If no one picks up across all three tiers, Clara will take a detailed message, including the urgency level, and send text alerts to all three contacts. For non-emergency calls after hours, Clara will take a message, collecting the caller's name, company, phone number, system type, and what service they need. The caller will be informed that Coastal Alarm Systems will follow up by 10 AM the next business day.
office_hours_flow_summary: During business hours (Monday-Friday, 8 AM-5 PM ET), all incoming calls are initially transferred to Maria (813-555-0145). If Maria is busy and does not answer within 20 seconds, Clara will handle the call by collecting the necessary information, and Maria will call the client back. For existing customers who specifically ask to speak with Tony, Clara will transfer the call directly to Tony's cell (813-555-0167). Calls from monitoring companies are considered high priority; Clara will attempt to transfer these calls to Maria immediately, and if Maria is unavailable, the call will be transferred to Tony.
special_rules: Coastal Alarm Systems observes major holidays but maintains flexibility for high-priority clients requiring emergency service.
The business exclusively handles commercial work; no residential services are provided.
Electrical work is limited to low-voltage systems; services like new panel or outlet installation are not offered.
The company does not sell or service safes, nor do they provide locksmithing services.

Clara is strictly prohibited from asking for or recording any alarm codes, passwords, or access credentials due to security protocols. Any related inquiries should be noted and routed to Maria or Tony.

Coastal Alarm Systems operates within the Tampa Bay metropolitan area, including Tampa, St. Petersburg, Clearwater, Brandon, and surrounding Hillsborough and Pinellas counties. Calls from outside this service area will not be accommodated.
Coastal Alarm Systems is licensed in Florida for alarm contractor work under State License EF20001567, which Clara can reference if asked.
notes: Updated with onboarding data. Operational details confirmed.
